Live Execution / Creative Practice Akara x FUBU / 2026

Final Output / GPT Image 2 / 16:9 / High Quality

One model. Three looks.
One pipeline.

Akara styled across three FUBU reimagined garments. The model identity is locked across every output. The garment is the variable. The pipeline runs the same way each time.

A brand brings a garment. The model identity is already built. The pipeline runs and the output is campaign-ready. No studio day, no photographer, no booking.

Process / In Order

01

Model Identity Lock

Akara base reference established in Higgsfield Soul ID. Face, skin tone, bone structure, and bob locked. ChatGPT seed used to anchor identity before any generation.

02

Garment Input

FUBU Technical Utility Jacket spec: ripstop shell, tonal embroidered logo, shockcord hem, hidden zip pockets. Translated into prompt language with fabric and silhouette detail.

03

Creative Direction

Giant-figure-over-city concept. Dramatic low aerial angle, city skyline below, cinematic blue sky, helicopters in distance. Camera looking up. Wind in hair. Plane in hand.

04

Generation / GPT Image 2

16:9 aspect ratio, 1K resolution, High quality. Face lock instruction included at prompt head. Ultra photorealistic, high contrast, warm skin against cool blue city sky.

05

Video / In Production

Hero frame moving to Gen-4 Video. Camera pulls back from her face across the skyline. Wind in jacket. Plane in hand. Motion without chaos.

Full Prompt / Documented

Identity lock: This model's face is locked. Reject any generation that alters her features. Black female model, deep rich ebony skin, warm undertones, high cheekbones, full lips, dark almond eyes, ear-length jet black bob.

Styling: Red FUBU utility jacket, cropped boxy ripstop shell, tonal red embroidered logo, shockcord hem, hidden zip pockets. Medium wash denim jorts, white clean sole sneakers.

Scene: Akara as a giant figure towering over a major city skyline, shot from a dramatic low aerial angle. One hand extended toward the lens with a plane in hand, pulling it out of the sky. Wind moves through her hair.

Settings: GPT Image 2 · 16:9 · 1K · High Quality

Open Issue
/ The Archive: Process and Execution

A polished campaign is only as good as the revisions it took to get there. I do not just generate. I reconstruct. Here is the raw work — adjusting garment assembly, fixing hallucinated seams, and locking character identity so the fashion stays authentic to the culture.

Iteration 01

Correcting Construction

The Raw Output

Initial generations frequently yield impossible sleeve attachments, incorrect hardware, and culturally stereotyped styling choices that do not match the brief.

The Refined Prompt

Close-up of reconstructed patchwork denim baby tee on a dress form, accurate straight stitch seams, industrial sewing machine in slate grey, neutral studio lighting, authentic 1990s silhouette, strictly no cultural caricatures.

The Final Asset

Accurate physical assembly with authentic cultural representation and no AI bias artifacts.

Iteration 02

Locking Character Identity

The Raw Output

Standard image models struggle to maintain consistent facial features and skin texture across different lighting setups and garment changes.

The Refined Prompt

FACE LOCK: do not alter skin tone, face shape, eye shape, lips, nose, jaw, or bone structure. Black woman, deep ebony skin, warm undertones, high cheekbones, jet black ear-length bob. Face and features identical to reference image throughout every frame.

The Final Asset

Consistent character identity preserved across the entire campaign workflow — jacket, jersey, and tracksuit outputs all holding the same face.

Angel Holmes 2026

001 / AI Model Development / @akaracali.official

Akara

AI Model Identity / Creative Direction / 2026

Higgsfield Soul ID Runway Gen-4 Image ChatGPT Seed HappyHorse 1.0

"Can you hold a person's identity consistent across multiple AI tools without losing what makes them distinct? Akara is the answer."

Creative Direction Rationale

Akara was built to answer a specific question: can you hold a person's identity consistent across multiple AI tools without losing what makes them distinct? Most AI character work fails at the detail level. The face shifts, the tone drifts, the energy changes between platforms. The direction for Akara was to anchor everything in specificity. California-rooted, SoCal and Central Valley culture. Rich brown skin, jet black bob, casual editorial. The creative choice to lock her identity through ChatGPT as seed reference before moving into Runway Gen-4 meant the character survived the pipeline instead of getting generalized by it.

Before / Higgsfield Soul ID

After / Runway Gen-4 and ChatGPT Seed

01

Foundation

Built on Higgsfield Soul ID. Established base identity, face, and visual consistency across multiple angles.

02

Render

Runway Gen-4 Image with enhanced prompts. ChatGPT used as seed reference to anchor exact identity.

03

Motion

Best Runway output used as starting frame for HappyHorse 1.0 video. Brought to life with natural movement and warm golden studio lighting.

002 / AI Campaign Production / 2026

Akara × FUBU

Three garments. One model. One pipeline. Campaign-ready.

Runway ML GPT Image 2 ChatGPT Seed Higgsfield Soul ID Runway Workflows

"Akara wearing FUBU. Three garments, three looks, one consistent face. Built entirely in AI without a studio or a shoot day."

The Brief

FUBU has three reimagined garments: a technical utility jacket, a tonal football jersey, and a colorblock tracksuit. The goal was to show what a campaign looks like on a real, consistent model across all three pieces. Akara is the model. Runway is the studio.

The Workflow

Akara's identity was locked using three reference angles fed into the Runway Workflow alongside each garment spec sheet. GPT Image 2 generated each campaign image with face lock instructions anchoring her features across every output. Three prompts. Three garments. Same face every time.

Runway Workflow / Akara x FUBU Pipeline / 3-node structure / 2026

Campaign Outputs

Left: Utility Jacket / Blue · Center: Football Jersey / Charcoal · Right: Tracksuit / FUBU Red + Black

01

Model Identity

Akara's face locked via ChatGPT seed and three-angle reference inputs. Deep ebony skin, jet black bob, high cheekbones — consistent across every output.

02

Garment Input

FUBU spec sheets used as visual reference nodes. Fabric details, silhouette, colorway — all fed directly into the generation prompt.

03

Pipeline Output

Three campaign images. Three distinct looks. One consistent model. Ready to hand to a brand team, a social strategist, or an ecommerce team.

What This Demonstrates

A brand with multiple SKUs needs campaign imagery for each one. Traditional production means separate shoot days and separate budgets. This pipeline takes a locked model and a garment spec and generates campaign-ready output in a single run. The brand gets visual consistency across every piece without a studio.

003 / Ecommerce Strategy / Customer Research

Nöz Ecommerce

Empathy mapping, Shopify architecture, full-funnel strategy

Shopify Empathy Mapping Customer Research Ecommerce Strategy

"Understanding the customer is the strategy. Everything else is execution."

Overview

Noz is a direct-to-consumer brand case study focused on building an ecommerce operation grounded in real customer insight. The work starts with research — who the customer is, what they actually need, and where the current experience fails them — then builds outward into Shopify architecture and product strategy.

Empathy Map

Built through customer interviews, social listening, and behavioral observation. Maps what the Noz customer thinks, feels, says, and does — and where those four things do not align. That gap is where the product and UX opportunity lives.

Key Findings

01

Discovery Gap

Customers find the product through social but cannot locate it on-site. Navigation and search are the first friction point.

02

Trust Signal Absence

No reviews, limited photography, and sparse product copy reduce conversion confidence — especially for first-time buyers.

03

Repeat Purchase Barrier

No loyalty mechanic or post-purchase communication keeping customers in the brand's orbit after the first order.
